threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You stand at the bus stop staring at the ever-changing digital timetable, clearly flummoxed when it shows 'delays due to tube strikes'.
Caught a guy on the 355 last night doing a spell from Hamlet out loud, full-on theatrical. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: On a rainy Tuesday, I saw a guy selling umbrellas made of old bicycle parts at Hermannplatz. That's when I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You'll find spontaneous dance parties in the U-Bahn at night. Just a bunch of strangers turning a platform into a dance floor, even with the BVG strikes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you can name at least three taco trucks that have closed and reopened in the last year alone.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit helping a tourist navigate the Muni app while dodging a street performer blasting opera. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars in Gràcia are full of hipsters, but dive into a corner tavern and you'll find locals reminiscing over the same gossip since '98.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they wait for the metro like it's a taxi. Locals know it's a game of patience, not speed.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when you see the bus routes don’t match your phone’s GPS. Locals just know to look up and follow the crowd.
Last night on the 88, a couple were debating whose turn it was to pay for the next round. Tipsy arguments over pint prices make for lively bus rides!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a neighbor fixing a bike with a coffee in one hand and a dog leash in the other, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice the random street parties that pop up after protests? Only in Berlin can a strike turn into a block party with DJ sets and barbecues.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they casually know the BART delays by heart. The tourists are the ones still standing confused at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo taking a nap on the 28 bus, with an open bottle of champagne beside him. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are for tight-knit gatherings, but on weekends, they're just an echo of competing music and stray dogs chasing each other.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still ask for tap water in restaurants; locals don’t bother anymore, just a bottle on the table.
